renewal_conditions: Written notice at least 12 months but not more than 18 months prior to expiration
Good standing, no breach or default
Sign then-current Franchise Agreement (may have materially different terms)
Meet then-current qualification and training requirements
Remodel Store to then-current standards at franchisee's expense
grant_and_term.territory: As defined in Exhibit A; up to 2-mile radius typical
grant_and_term.search_area: Non-exclusive search area if no approved location at signing

fees_and_payment.royalty: 6% of weekly Net Sales, due 3rd business day of each week
fees_and_payment.brand_marketing_fee: 1% of weekly Net Sales (can increase to 2% on 90 days notice)
fees_and_payment.estimated_fee_debit: If Net Sales not timely reported, franchisor can debit estimated amount
fees_and_payment.interest: Lesser of 1.5%/month or highest rate allowed by applicable law

site_selection_and_construction.site_deadline: 2 months from FA signing to agree on site; failure = termination
site_selection_and_construction.opening_deadline: 6 months from FA signing or 4 months from site approval
site_selection_and_construction.construction: Must use franchisor-designated or approved architect, engineer, and general contractor
site_selection_and_construction.plans_approval: All plans require franchisor approval; cannot modify without written approval
site_selection_and_construction.failure_consequences: Termination and retention of all amounts paid

training_obligations.initial_training: Up to 3 trainees at no fee; additional trainees at $2,000/person (can increase 25%)
training_obligations.completion_required: Must satisfy pre-opening training requirements 30 days before scheduled opening
training_obligations.failure_consequence: May terminate FA; no reimbursement of IFF or training fees
training_obligations.refresher_training: May require up to 4 days/person/year plus 3 days conventions
training_obligations.replacement_training: Must enroll replacement Designated Principal/GM promptly; franchisor may charge fee
training_obligations.self_training_after_3rd_store: For 4th+ ADA store, franchisee trains own personnel per franchisor standards

non_curable_defaults: Fraud, felony conviction, or guilty/no contest plea
Loss of premises or required license/permit
Non-compete breach or failure to obtain similar covenants from staff
Repeated default after cure (same default again)
3+ defaults within 12 months (whether or not cured)
termination_triggers_and_cure.curable_defaults_7_days: Monetary defaults, refuse inspection/audit, not operating during required hours

transfer_restrictions.transfer_definition: Transfer of any rights, obligations, material assets, or ownership interest
conditions_transferee: Sign new FA (may be materially different)
transfer_restrictions.rofr: Franchisor can match any third-party offer (Section 15.6)
transfer_restrictions.death: Assign to approved party within 12 months (Section 15.7)
transfer_restrictions.disability: Assign within 6 months of permanent disability notice (Sections 15.8, 15.9)

during_term.scope: No involvement in any Competitive Business (>50% tea sales or same/similar to Gong cha)
during_term.family_extension: Immediate family member engaging in Competitive Business = default

post_term.geographic: 5-mile radius from Store location or any other Gong cha store
post_term.public_company_exception: Less than 5% beneficial interest in publicly listed corporation

noncompete_scope_and_duration.personal_covenants: Must obtain similar covenants from all managers/personnel who receive training or confidential information (Exhibit E form)
noncompete_scope_and_duration.non_dealing: Cannot engage franchisor's suppliers for direct supply of Products outside the FA (Section 18.10)
noncompete_scope_and_duration.enforcement: Franchisee pays all enforcement costs; franchisor entitled to injunctive relief

guaranty_scope.scope: All Principals must sign. Guaranty covers all obligations under FA.
guaranty_scope.spousal: Spouse must sign (flagged as Special Risk in FDD cover)

liquidated_damages.prohibited_product_fee: $250/day for each day unapproved product/service offered
liquidated_damages.notes: Can increase 25% over FA term. CA addendum notes this may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code §1671.

mediation.location: Within 5 miles of franchisor's principal place of business (Boston, MA)
mediation.exception: IP disputes, monetary obligations, injunctive relief
mediation.cost_sharing: Each party bears own costs; split mediator cost

arbitration.location: Within 5 miles of franchisor's principal place of business
arbitration.injunctive_relief: Preserved for either party pending arbitration

required_coverages: All-risks insurance (full replacement cost of FF&E, inventory, leasehold improvements)
insurance_requirements.franchisor_as_insured: Must name franchisor as additional named insured
insurance_requirements.certificates: 30 days before opening; annually on renewal
insurance_requirements.failure_consequence: Franchisor may obtain coverage and charge franchisee

indemnification.scope: Claims arising from Store operations, franchisee's breach, or franchisee's acts/omissions

release_at_signing.description: By executing the FA, franchisee releases franchisor from all claims prior to and including the effective date
release_at_signing.scope: All obligations, debts, liabilities, demands, claims, actions, damage — known or unknown
release_at_signing.notes: This is a release at initial signing, not just at renewal/transfer. Subject to state law limitations (CA §31512).

force_majeure.notes: No explicit force majeure clause identified in the FA text extraction.

distinctive_or_unusual_clauses.description: Franchisee may not use confidential information for machine learning, AI model training, algorithm improvement, or similar activities. May not input confidential information into any generative AI platform.
If franchisor determines amendments needed for competition law compliance, franchisee must execute supplemental agreement 'forthwith on demand'.
Immediate family (spouse, domestic partner, parent, child) engaging in a Competitive Business triggers default even if they are not personally subject to the covenants.
Upon executing the FA, franchisee releases all claims against franchisor prior to effective date. This is at initial signing, not only at renewal.
Franchisor's exercise of rights/discretion not subject to limitation or review. Good faith covenant cannot imply obligations inconsistent with FA terms.
